title: Monday Community Private Messages disabled!
excerpt:
Hi, i cant figure where i can PM someone… even my previous PM and conversations with community members cant be found… 
Please some of Monday.com can assist me?

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, i cant figure where i can PM someone… even my previous PM and conversations with community members cant be found…

Please some of Monday.com can assist me?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @hlopezvc,

Sorry about this! If you select your profile in the top right hand corner, and click on the messages icon, do you have the option to send a new message to someone? Let us know!

Best,
Bianca

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
BiancaT:

e

i dont see msg icon anymore!!!

image1182×575 86.1 KB

where it would be?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Reached out to Juliette to confirm whether this option has been removed from user’s profiles…will update once I hear back…

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for sending this screenshot across, and we apologize for the confusion caused here! I was able to follow up internally, and it has been confirmed that the feature has been removed for the time being, in an attempt to encourage our users to engage with each other within the open forum That being said, we are certainly open to feedback, and if the DM feature is a valuable addition to the platform, please let us know as we are always looking to improve our community!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@BiancaT

I strongly believe that the DM feature is important to have. Although I often redirect people to post communications to me into the open forum, not every communication should be public. It is a severe limitation to not be able to directly reach out to people anymore.

Please reactivate this feature.

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
Definitely agree with the sentiments of @hlopezvc and @JCorrell.

There are conversions in DMs that need to be revisited and or continued and monday.com’s decision to just remove without any head-ups doesn’t speak well of the company.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hopefully monday․com will decide to re-enable this way for everyone to connect directly. As others have said - not every conversation should be public and one feature of a true community is strong relationship building and networking. Not just public Q&A.

~ Deb Cinkus, PMP
Certified monday․com Partner
CEO & Principal Consultant, Polished Geek

Want to chat live with other monday․com fans & users?
Join our unofficial community chat on Discord: The Monday Neighborhood

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Im certanly need sometimes to chat with someone in the forum without needing to create a topic, there are specific questions that doesnt need to be public.
Most anoying thing is that this wasnt announced, its was done directly.

Please re-activate this function.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @BiancaT

Looks like this forum inherits the change process from monday.com core product: make changes without telling anyone. This is a very bad practice and I agree with the other here that we need the DM function re-activated.

On a side note: when posting to the “Share you monday apps!” subforum the posts does not show up (due to moderation?). Moderation is a good thing but it is taking a very long time and monday support does not even know who to ping to moderate the post. All of this does not help getting more involvement with the community.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
@BiancaT - I echo the comments by my peers in the thread. This needs to be added back in as not all conversations are public. We as partners and general helpers to the community, sometimes we share details such as URLs and sensitive data, etc.

Please add this back asap.
Mark

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Everyone!

So sorry about the inconvenience. We will make sure to better communicate changes to our community moving forward. I will return with an update about this ASAP as we are revamping our community forum with some awesome updates and this feature was temporarily turned off. So sorry about the negative impact and will update shortly since this was very important to many of our community members!

Thanks for the feedback!

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
@kolaai @JCorrell @basdebruin @mark.anley @PolishedGeek

Thanks for your feedback! We have returned the ability for users to send each other PM for our engaged users. Moderators are unable to respond to private individual messages and it is always preferable to post/comment and engage with each other on the public forum so everyone in the community can get involved and learn from each other.

Also please keep in mind, we are creating some awesome changes and a new look for our community in the coming weeks so think may look a little different as we update. We will keep you informed as we release these changes and would love your feedback.

For any problems in the future related to community features, please feel free to reach out. Thanks for being active members of our community. We appreciate you!
